title: Eval
description: The final evaluation for one job. Written ONCE by the `evaluate` stage. This file is a source of truth: every downstream artifact (daily report, resume, cover letter, deep report, application answers) reads this file and never re-scores the job. Rubric and matching methodology are adapted from Career Ops; the JSON-only output (no long markdown) is CareerOS's own choice, made so the daily pipeline stays cheap.

id.description: Same id as the Job it evaluates.

score.description: Weighted average of the rubric dimensions, rounded to 1 decimal.

strengths.description: Exactly the top 3 — deliberately capped so the daily report stays scannable.

ats_keywords.description: Keywords surfaced from the JD, used downstream to guide resume tailoring — never invented, only lifted from the JD text.

company_summary.description: 3 concise lines about the company. Exists so the Level-1 daily report needs zero extra AI calls — it is rendered directly from this field.

fit_paragraph.description: One paragraph (<=80 words), why this is or isn't a fit. Doubles as the spine of the cover letter.

rubric.description: Career Ops-derived weighted rubric. Each dimension is 0-5; score is their weighted average.

prompt_version.description: Which prompts/eval_vN.md produced this, e.g. 'v1'. Part of the cache fingerprint.

profile_version.description: profile.yaml's own version field at eval time. Part of the cache fingerprint — bump the profile, evals recompute.

job_hash.description: sha1 of the Job's content. Part of the cache fingerprint — if the posting is byte-identical to a prior run, the cached eval is reused.
